name: REACTIVE ARTHRITIS
definition: REITER SYNDROME noninfectious, asymmetric inflammatory oligoarthropathy characterized by the triad of
dominant_finding: articular soft-tissue swelling + joint space narrowing in 50% (particularly knee, ankle, foot)
discriminating_features: articular soft-tissue swelling + joint space narrowing in 50% (particularly knee, ankle, foot)
widening + inflammation of Achilles + plantar fascial tendons → heel swelling +
"fluffy" periosteal reaction (DISTINCTIVE) at metatarsal necks, proximal phalanges,

general: articular soft-tissue swelling + joint space narrowing in 50% (particularly knee, ankle, foot)
widening + inflammation of Achilles + plantar fascial tendons → heel swelling +
"fluffy" periosteal reaction (DISTINCTIVE) at metatarsal necks, proximal phalanges,
calcaneal spur at insertion of plantar fascia + Achilles tendon
marginal erosions, loss of joint space
bilateral sacroiliac changes indistinguishable from ankylosing / psoriatic spondylitis (in
paravertebral ossification = isolated "floating osteophyte" usually in thoracolumbar area

clinical.Cx: gastric ulcer + hemorrhage; aortic incompetence; heart block; amyloidosis
